title: Thelypteris austrophilippina
intro: Thelypteris austrophilippina, also known as the Philippine shield fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of the Philippines and surrounding islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris austrophilippina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris austrophilippina is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used to make infusions and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris austrophilippina is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which consists of approximately 150 species of ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The classification of Thelypteris austrophilippina is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Pteridophyta. This fern species is native to the Philippines.
